_note: TEMPLATE. Copy to config/devices.local.json (gitignored) and describe YOUR fleet: each ESP32 board and which plant sits on each capacitive channel. This is the multi-device registry (#485) the monitor-all view (#486) reads to attribute N devices' streams to the 11 plants. The JSON shape (schema_version 1) is RATIFIED (Trellis 2026-07-03, #583/#300). Per-device card-header fields (#583, ADR-0020): 'name' = friendly identity (re-nameable, never a MAC; falls back to a legacy 'label'); 'hostname' = the synthetic '.local' name (null for a tethered device — the card shows its serial port instead; the xxxx is the device's install-suffix, not a MAC). Order in this array is the dashboard's first-seen card order — never re-sorted by state. Optional per-device 'base_url' (the served root, e.g. http://192.168.1.42): when set, the live dashboard also polls that device's GET /telemetry (#276) — omit it for tethered/serial-only devices (the example ships with none so a fresh checkout never blocks polling a placeholder address). Consumers depend on device_registry.py's functions, not this file's layout. Placeholder values below.
